_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
This section maps the binding EU instruments currently published on EUR-Lex that are most material to the workspace's operations. This is not a full compliance analysis of any instrument. It is a source-anchored index for triage and prioritisation. Each instrument is filtered by transport mode and cargo vertical relevance.

## ReFuelEU Aviation — Regulation (EU) 2023/2405

**What it does:** Mandates that fuel suppliers at EU airports blend increasing minimum percentages of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) into jet fuel supplied to aircraft operators. Blending obligations began 2% on 1 January 2025, scaling to 6% by 2030 and 70% by 2050. Synthetic fuels sub-mandate applies from 2030. *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/2405, EUR-Lex / Official Journal, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/2405/oj/eng.*

**Effect on the workspace:** Air is the workspace's primary transport mode. All EU airport departures by airline partners are subject to blending requirements. SAF costs are currently materially higher than conventional jet fuel — cost differential requires carrier-specific data to quantify surcharge exposure. The workspace should request transparency from airline partners on SAF surcharge methodology. *Source: EUR-Lex summary, ReFuelEU Aviation — Sustainable Air Transport, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/EN/legal-content/summary/refueleu-aviation-sustainable-air-transport.html. EASA Digital Reporting Tool, https://www.easa.europa.eu/en/domains/environment/refueleu-aviation-digital-reporting-tool.*

**Cargo vertical filtering:** Highest cost exposure for air-intensive verticals: live events, fine art, film and TV, high-value automotive. All rely heavily on air freight for time-sensitive and high-value moves on Europe lanes.

## FuelEU Maritime — Regulation (EU) 2023/1805

**What it does:** Establishes greenhouse gas intensity limits on energy used by vessels calling at EU ports (gross tonnage ≥5,000 GT), phasing down from a 2% reduction vs 2020 baseline from 1 January 2025, to 80% reduction by 2050. Requires vessels to connect to onshore power supply at EU ports from 2030 (for applicable vessel categories). *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/1805, EUR-Lex / Official Journal, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1805/oj/eng. Full CELEX text: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R1805.*

**Effect on the workspace:** Ocean is the workspace's tertiary mode. For any shipments moved on ocean via EU port calls, vessel operators bear the compliance obligation. However, the workspace will see cost pass-through as vessel operators acquire compliant fuel blends or purchase EU ETS allowances. Cost impact on specific routes requires carrier-specific data. *Source: EC DG CLIMA Shipping, https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/transport-decarbonisation/reducing-emissions-shipping-sector_en.*

**Cargo vertical filtering:** Humanitarian and high-value automotive verticals are most likely to use ocean on applicable routes. Fine art and live events predominantly use air, limiting FuelEU Maritime exposure for those verticals.

## EU ETS — Maritime Inclusion (EU ETS Phase 4 extension)

**What it does:** From 1 January 2024, shipping was phased into the EU Emissions Trading System. Vessel operators must surrender EU Allowances (EUAs) for verified CO2 emissions from voyages to/from EU ports. Phase-in: 40% of reported emissions in 2024, 70% in 2025, 100% from 2026. *Source: EEA EU ETS Data Viewer, European Environment Agency, https://www.eea.europa.eu/en/analysis/maps-and-charts/emissions-trading-viewer-1-dashboards. Source: EC DG CLIMA Shipping, https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/transport-decarbonisation/reducing-emissions-shipping-sector_en.*

**Effect on the workspace:** 2026 marks 100% surrender obligation — the largest step-change in ETS cost exposure for shipping. Workspace ocean-mode clients and carriers will face the highest ETS-linked surcharge levels from 2026 onward. EUA prices are publicly trackable via the EEA Data Viewer. The workspace can use this data to validate carrier surcharge claims.

## PPWR — Regulation (EU) 2025/40

**What it does:** Replaces the Packaging and Packaging Waste Directive. Introduces binding recyclability requirements, recycled content targets, restrictions on certain packaging formats, and Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) obligations for producers who place packaging on the EU market. Published in the Official Journal 22 January 2025; entry into force 12 February 2025. *Source: Regulation (EU) 2025/40, Publications Office of the European Union / EUR-Lex, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2025/40/oj/eng. Full legal text: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202500040.*

**Effect on the workspace:** Legal Confirmation Required on whether the workspace's role in commissioning or importing specialised packaging for live events, fine art, or high-value automotive cargo on EU trade lanes constitutes "placing packaging on the EU market" under PPWR. If it does, EPR registration and reporting obligations apply directly to the workspace in each EU Member State where packaging is placed.

## CBAM — Regulation (EU) 2023/956

**What it does:** Establishes a Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism requiring importers of covered goods (currently: cement, iron and steel, aluminium, fertilisers, electricity, hydrogen) to declare embedded emissions and surrender CBAM certificates. Transitional reporting period ran 2023–2025. Definitive phase begins 1 January 2026. *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/956, EUR-Lex / Official Journal, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R0956. EC CBAM Portal, https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism_en. DG TAXUD CBAM Guidance, https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/carbon-border-adjustment-mechanism/cbam-legislation-and-guidance_en.*

**Effect on the workspace:** The workspace's cargo verticals (live events, fine art, luxury goods, film and TV, high-value automotive, humanitarian) do not, in their standard form, fall within CBAM's current covered sectors. However, where the workspace acts as customs broker for clients importing covered goods — including aluminium components or steel structures used in live events or automotive — CBAM declaration obligations may apply to the filing party or importer of record. Legal Confirmation Required on filing-party obligations under CBAM when the workspace acts as customs representative.

## Union Customs Code — Regulation (EU) No 952/2013

**What it does:** The UCC is the foundational customs law of the EU, governing all customs procedures for goods imported into or exported from the EU. It defines the obligations of declarants, customs representatives, and holders of customs authorisations. *Source: Regulation (EU) No 952/2013, EUR-Lex / Official Journal, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32013R0952.*

**Effect on the workspace:** As a freight forwarder acting as customs representative, the workspace operates under the UCC on every EU import and export. ICS2 obligations (pre-arrival safety and security filings for air freight into the EU) are derived from UCC authority. *Source: ICS2 portal, European Commission DG TAXUD, https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/customs/customs-security/import-control-system-2_en. ICS2 FAQ, https://taxation-customs.ec.europa.eu/customs/customs-security/import-control-system-2/faq_en.*

## CO2 Standards for Heavy-Duty Vehicles — Regulations (EU) 2019/1242 and 2024/1610

**What it does:** Sets CO2 emission performance standards for new heavy-duty vehicles (trucks, buses, coaches). Regulation (EU) 2024/1610 amended and strengthened these standards. Obligations fall on vehicle manufacturers, not on road operators or freight forwarders. *Source: Regulation (EU) 2019/1242, EUR-Lex, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32019R1242. Regulation (EU) 2024/1610, EUR-Lex, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=OJ:L_202401610. EC DG CLIMA lorries page, https://climate.ec.europa.eu/eu-action/transport-decarbonisation/road-transport/lorries-buses-and-coaches_en.*

**Effect on the workspace:** The workspace does not manufacture vehicles. Indirect effect: road carriers the workspace contracts will face fleet renewal pressures as vehicle CO2 standards tighten, which may affect road freight availability and pricing on European trade lanes over the medium term. Effect on specific road freight rates requires carrier-specific data.

**Cargo vertical filtering:** Road is the workspace's secondary mode. Live events, fine art, and high-value automotive all use road transport within Europe. Fleet renewal pressure is most visible for long-haul European road moves.

## Alternative Fuels Infrastructure — Regulation (EU) 2023/1804 (AFIR)

**What it does:** Mandates minimum deployment of EV charging and hydrogen refuelling infrastructure along the TEN-T network. Primary obligations fall on Member States and infrastructure operators. *Source: Regulation (EU) 2023/1804, EUR-Lex / Official Journal, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2023/1804/oj/eng. CELEX consolidated version: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX:32023R1804. EC DG MOVE AFIR page, https://transport.ec.europa.eu/transport-themes/clean-transport/alternative-fuels-sustainable-mobility-europe/alternative-fuels-infrastructure_en.*

**Effect on the workspace:** AFIR creates the infrastructure precondition for zero-emission road freight on TEN-T corridors. The workspace does not bear AFIR obligations directly. Relevance is strategic: as AFIR infrastructure deploys, road carriers will face accelerating pressure to electrify fleets, and the workspace's road freight procurement will need to accommodate electric or hydrogen vehicle availability windows. This is a medium-term planning input, not a near-term compliance obligation.

## EU Transport Emissions Accounting — COM(2023) 441

**What it does:** Proposed regulation to establish a harmonised EU framework for measuring and reporting GHG emissions from transport services, covering all modes. Political agreement reached between European Parliament and Council in November 2025. Formal adoption and OJ publication pending as of April 2026. *Source: COM(2023) 441 final, EUR-Lex, https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=comnat:COM_2023_0441_FIN. European Parliament Legislative Train Schedule, https://www.europarl.europa.eu/legislative-train/spotlight-JD%2023-24/file-measurement-of-transport-and-logistics-emissions. EC DG MOVE press release, November 2025, https://transport.ec.europa.eu/news-events/news/commission-welcomes-political-agreement-new-rules-harmonise-transport-emissions-calculations-eu-2025-11-06_en.*

**Effect on the workspace:** Once formally adopted and in force, this regulation will impose direct obligations on the workspace as a transport service provider to supply clients with harmonised GHG emissions data per shipment, across all modes. This is a direct workspace obligation — not a pass-through from a carrier. For workspaces currently operating without mode-level emissions calculation infrastructure, the gap between current state and the regulation's requirements is material. Legal Confirmation Required on the exact definition of "transport service provider" in the final adopted text, which has not yet been published in the OJ as of April 2026.

---
